heading: Traumatic brain injury: comprehensive program for long-term rehabilitation
label: 1710D Traumatic brain injury: comprehensive program for long-term rehabilitation

children.heading: .—Comprehensive Program
children.label: a .—Comprehensive Program
children.text: In developing plans for the rehabilitation and reintegration of individuals with traumatic brain injury under , the Secretary shall develop and carry out a comprehensive program of long-term care and rehabilitative services (as defined in ) for post-acute traumatic brain injury rehabilitation that includes residential, community, and home-based components utilizing interdisciplinary teams.section 1710C of this titlesection 1710C of this title

children.heading: .—Location of Program
children.label: b .—Location of Program
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out the program developed under subsection (a) in each Department polytrauma rehabilitation center designated by the Secretary.

served on active duty in a theater of combat operations (as determined by the Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of Defense) during a period of war after the Persian Gulf War, or in combat against a hostile force during a period of hostilities after ;November 11, 1998
is diagnosed as suffering from moderate to severe traumatic brain injury; and
is unable to manage routine activities of daily living without supervision or assistance, as determined by the Secretary.
A description of the operation of the program.
The number of veterans provided care under the program during the year preceding such report.
The cost of operating the program during the year preceding such report.
